description: Leaner spec for gpt-5-nano. Fewer tokens, tighter rules.

categories.name: HIGH_CONFIDENCE
categories.description: Explicit evidence of imports AND international shipping beyond US/Canada/Mexico.

categories.name: MEDIUM_CONFIDENCE
categories.description: Strong import inference + explicit international shipping evidence.

categories.name: DISQUALIFIED
categories.description: No physical products, no imports, OR no explicit international shipping proof.

questions.question: Does this company sell physical products?
questions.guidance: YES: tangible products (apparel, tools, electronics, machinery). NO: software, consulting, digital-only.

questions.question: Does this company import goods into the US?
questions.guidance: YES if: 'Made in China/Vietnam/overseas', imports mentioned. YES by inference: apparel/shoes/electronics without 'Made in USA'. NO if: 'Made in USA', print-on-demand, screen printing. NO if: just buying from US distributors.

questions.question: Does this company ship to countries OTHER than US/Canada/Mexico?
questions.guidance: REQUIRE EXPLICIT PROOF: 'ships worldwide', shipping page lists Europe/Asia, country selector at checkout. Canada/Mexico ONLY = NO. No shipping info = NO. Do NOT infer.

questions.question: Is this company a brand owner or direct importer?
questions.guidance: YES: own brand products, imports directly. NO: buys from US distributors, mainstream retail brands only.

questions.question: What international shipping evidence was found?

classification_logic.description: Require explicit export evidence to qualify.
simple_rules: Rule 3: If ships_internationally=false (or no explicit proof) → DISQUALIFIED
Rule 4: If imports=true AND explicit intl shipping=true → MEDIUM_CONFIDENCE or higher
classification_logic.critical_constraint: ships_internationally requires EXPLICIT evidence - do not infer.

sufficiency_check.prompt_addition: CRITICAL: ships_internationally needs EXPLICIT evidence. Canada/Mexico alone = NO.
